verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-lumenal chaperone with no TM domain — baseline localization is strictly intracellular (ER lumen). However, under ER stress conditions characteristic of tumor microenvironments (hypoxia, nutrient deprivation, oncogenic transformation) and viral infection, HSPA5 translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Surface GRP78 (sGRP78) is robustly documented by FACS, surface biotinylation, and anti-GRP78 antibodies (e.g., MAb159) that bind live tumor cells but not normal fibroblasts. Multiple clinical-stage surface-directed antibody and peptide-drug programs (MAb159, Pep42) target cell-surface GRP78 on tumor cells. This qualifies as contextual / cell_state_induced (oncogenic transformation + ER stress).
